title: Antonio Negri, Spinoza, Marx, and Digital Capitalism
author: Fuchs

opening_move.description: Spinoza's philosophy has been used by Negri to analyse capitalism and the working class, but it has not been adequately applied to digital capitalism specifically. The paper asks: how can Spinoza help us better understand digital capitalism, particularly the antagonism between digital labour and digital capital?
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Spinoza's concepts of potentia and potestas, combined with Marx's class analysis and Negri's digital/communicative capitalism, provide a framework for understanding digital capitalism's antagonisms as an antagonism between digital potentia (collective creative force) and digital potestas (domination).

theoretical_positioning.description: The paper critically and constructively engages Negri's Spinoza interpretation, combining it with Marx's class analysis to produce a framework for digital capitalism. It maps Spinoza's potentia/potestas dialectic onto digital labour/capital antagonism, digital commons/commodities, and productive forces/relations of production.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: Philosophical synthesis of Spinoza, Marx, and Negri; conceptual mapping of potentia/potestas onto digital capitalism categories.
epistemic_mode.notes: Fully normative: argument is philosophical synthesis. References to digital platforms are conceptual illustrations of the framework, not empirical cases.

normative_stake.domain: Marxist theory / digital capitalism / Spinoza / labour

confidence_notes: Type 2 (framework construction / synthesis): combines Spinoza, Marx, and Negri to generate a new analytical vocabulary for digital capitalism. No empirical case. Open conclusion because the framework is proposed but not fully applied. Typical of PSC's political philosophy + Marxist theory strand.
